root_cause: Worker lint delta compares full-repo eslint output against an empty lint baseline (.index/verify/lint_baseline.json, 0 issues) without scope_paths filtering. That flags 888 pre-existing @meriter/web warnings as new. The same 888 warnings appear with or without the write_set changes (verified by reverting api/package.json and re-running web lint). None of the 888 issues are in write_set paths (api/package.json, pnpm-lock.yaml).
actions_taken: Restored eslint-plugin-import (^2.32.0) and eslint-import-resolver-typescript (^4.4.4) in api/package.json after accidental revert during diagnosis
Ran pnpm install to refresh pnpm-lock.yaml
Re-verified pnpm install (exit 0), api lint (exit 0, no import/resolver errors), and scoped lint delta (new_scoped=0)
verifier_expectation: Node success criteria satisfied. Repair/orchestrator lint channel should pass (scope_paths=write_set yields new_count=0). Worker attempt lint may still report 888 vs empty baseline until harness applies scope_paths to worker run_lint_delta or baseline is seeded with legacy web debt; that is outside write_set.
